_meta.issue: #112-#118 [M15] / #124 [M16] Formative path S4 — Holak Scale (training module, expansion)
_meta.interaction: Practice classification (kind: classify) — formative exercise, NOT scored toward completion
_meta.source: Holak Scale maturity axes: context management, token economy, repeatable workflows (skills). Attribution: quality-blog.eu/pl/blog/skala-holaka.
_meta.dataPolicy: ALL examples SYNTHETIC and generic (no real data/PII). Formative module — no pass threshold, no certificate.

intro: You spot higher AI maturity not by the number of tools, but by HOW you feed the model context, what it costs, and whether you can turn a repeatable task into a skill. This module trains context hygiene, token-cost awareness, and building repeatable workflows. It is a growth exercise, not a test.

blocks.text: The model sees only what fits in the context window. Too little context → a generic or wrong answer; too much irrelevant content → the model loses the signal and token cost grows. Mature practice means giving exactly what is needed — no more, no less.

items: Context = the data, instructions and examples that truly affect the task.
Token cost grows with every character pasted — including the useless ones.
Repeatable tasks gain the most from templates and context hygiene.

blocks.title: A Holak Scale axis
blocks.text: Deliberate management of context and cost is one of the axes that separate „I use AI” from „I use AI maturely”.
Lean practice selects context and reuses proven templates. Wasteful practice pastes „everything just in case”, mixes topics, and describes the context from scratch every time.

rows: Only fragments relevant to the task
The whole knowledge base „just in case”
A prompt template with slots for data
From scratch each time, in different words
Keep dozens of topics in one chat
blocks.textAlt: Comparison of lean and wasteful practices across three dimensions: context selection, repeatability, hygiene.

blocks.title: The „just in case” trap
blocks.text: Pasting your whole knowledge base into every prompt does not raise accuracy — more often it blurs the task and raises cost. Dose context deliberately.

screens.title: Skills: a repeatable workflow instead of a one-off prompt

blocks.text: A skill is a workflow described once: an instruction for „when and how” to do a typical task, with fixed context, output format, and verification steps. Instead of inventing a prompt every time, you run a proven recipe — cheaper, faster and more consistent.

items: Name the task and the trigger (when to use this skill).
Fix the context and output format (what you provide, what should come back).
Add a step to verify the result before you use it.
Save it as a template/skill and improve it after each use.

blocks.title: Why this saves context
blocks.text: A skill encodes once what you would otherwise describe repeatedly — fixed, deliberate context replaces ad-hoc pasting and lowers token cost at steady quality.

Next you will classify practices as lean or wasteful — including practices for building skills. There is no pass threshold; the order of items is random, so content matters, not a pattern. The goal is to recognize a pattern you can carry over into your own work.

interaction.title: Lean or wasteful context practice?
interaction.intro: Sort each practice. This is a growth exercise — feedback explains why a practice is (not) lean.

categories.label: Lean practice
categories.desc: Selects context, controls cost, reuses templates/skills.

categories.label: Wasteful practice
categories.desc: Adds excess, mixes topics, describes everything from scratch.

items.text: I pass only the document fragments relevant to the task.
items.rationale: Selecting context raises accuracy and lowers cost.

items.text: I paste my whole knowledge base into every prompt „just in case”.
items.rationale: Excess blurs the signal and raises token cost.

items.text: I keep a repeatable task as a prompt template with slots for data.
items.rationale: A template means repeatability and context hygiene.

items.text: I describe the context from scratch every time, in different words.
items.rationale: No template = wasted time and inconsistent results.

items.text: I have a skill (a described workflow) for my most frequent AI task.
items.rationale: A skill encodes fixed context and steps — cheaper and more consistent.

items.text: I keep dozens of unrelated topics in a single chat.
items.rationale: Mixing topics clutters the context and confuses the model.

items.text: I remove stale and contradictory information from the context before asking.
items.rationale: Clean context lowers the risk of error and hallucination.

items.text: I copy a long conversation history into a new task without selection.
items.rationale: Carried-over excess raises cost and injects irrelevant context.

points: Give exactly the context that affects the task — no more.
Turn repeatable tasks into templates and skills with fixed context and a verification step.
Every pasted character has a cost — dose context deliberately.
checklist: Can I tell which parts of the context are truly needed?
Do I have a skill/template for my most frequent AI task?
Do I clear stale and contradictory content from the context?
summary.nextStep: Next step: take one repeatable task and turn it into a skill — a template with fixed context, output format, and a verification step.
